definition: EM dollar vulnerability has migrated from sovereign to corporate balance sheets. Local currency share of EM sovereign debt rose from ~55% (2012) to ~75% (2025), but EM corporate dollar borrowing grew from ~$1.2T to ~$3.5T. Sovereign-focused vulnerability metrics now appear improved while systemic risk may not have declined.
evidence: BIS data: EM sovereign local currency share ~55% (2012) → ~75% (2025)
Petrobras, PEMEX, Chinese property developers validated mechanism during 2015 commodity downturn
pair_2 called this the single most valuable novel insight across both analyses
Sovereign metrics improved but system may not be safer
open_questions: What is the current maturity profile of EM corporate dollar debt?
Which sectors/countries have the highest concentration of unhedged corporate dollar exposure?
Does corporate dollar debt create the same forced-selling dynamics as sovereign crises?
